title: The Best Posts By Pittsburgh Businesses
subtitle: Instagram posts the businesses themselves made this quarter, ranked by likes. Two per business max. The counterpart to the creator-coverage list.
angle: the businesses that showed up on their own feed this quarter. What they posted, what worked.
intro:
Forty-five posts. Fifty-six thousand, seven hundred and forty-nine likes. Nearly a million plays. That is what Pittsburgh small businesses generated when they actually posted this spring, and 23 of them made this list. The thesis we have been tracking, that creators are filming this city while most of its businesses stay quiet, still holds. But this is the counterpart: the operators who showed up, and what happened when they did.

What worked, specifically, was product and place. Page's Dairy Mart in Arlington dropped a Pickle Split Sundae announcement that pulled 12,699 likes and 482,700 plays, the single most-engaged post on this entire list. Jeni's in Larimer put up a late-night Gooey Butter Cake video with a caption so plainspoken it felt almost accidental, 8,297 likes. Allegheny Coffee in the Strip thanked the workers clearing Penn Avenue and got something rarer than virality: goodwill.

The businesses here did not outsource their storytelling. No agency voice, no polished brand language. They wrote like people who work in their own shops, and that is exactly why the numbers moved. In Issue 02, we will look at whether that pattern holds through summer, when the foot traffic gets easier and the excuses to stay quiet get thinner.
